title: Judge by the Cover
description: Customize your written books, Dye them like leather armor! Or change the style entirely!
body:
# Judge by the cover Data/Resourcepack

### What is the JBTC datapack?

Have you ever written a masterpiece of a book (in minecraft), and thought the basic boring brown color of the cover was not cutting it?

With this Datapack you can finally judge a book by its cover!   (because the cover is customizable)

There are no functions in this datapack.

## Features:

  ### Dyeable covers -
   Place a Book & quill or a finished book in a crafting tabe with any combination of dyes.
   The book will change color the same way as leather gear.

   Dyed book & quills don't lose color when signed. Dyed written books that are copied share the origanal color with the copy.

   ### Binding styles -
   Place a Written Book in a smithing table with the material corresponding with the bind you want. your book will get a fancy new decoration!

   **Currently available binding styles:**
-    Belt - crafted with leather or rabbit hide
- Silver - crafted with a iron nugget
- Gilded - crafted with a gold nugget
- Chain - crafted with a chain (duh)
- Web - crafted with a cobweb

Each bind was created with roleplay/lore in mind, use your imagination!

## Planned features:

  ### Cauldron washing -
  Undye by right clicking a cauldron
  ### Chiseled Bookshelf/Lectern Display -
  Custom books will show in chiseled bookshelfs. the pixel art possabilities are endless 
  ### Make Enchantment glint toggleable -
  The default is off (its sorta ugly and makes it hard to see your custom cover) maybe it will be its own bind/cover style?

  (custom gui and re-editing signed books may also be added)
